name: Accessibility (a11y)
description: WCAG 2.1 AA rules for AI assistants building web interfaces. Ensures alt text, keyboard navigation, ARIA usage, and colour contrast are addressed in every UI task.
author: Instill AI

preferences.rule: Every `` must have a descriptive `alt` attribute. Decorative images use `alt=""` with `aria-hidden="true"`.
All interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) must be reachable and operable via keyboard alone. Never suppress focus outlines without providing a visible alternative.
Use semantic HTML first (nav, main, article, button, label) before reaching for ARIA. ARIA supplements; it does not replace semantics.
Colour contrast must meet WCAG AA: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text and UI components. Never convey information by colour alone.
Form inputs must have associated `<label>` elements or `aria-label`. Placeholder text is not a label.
Dynamic content updates (toasts, modals, errors) must use `aria-live` regions or focus management so screen reader users are notified.
Skip-navigation links must be the first focusable element on every page.
